personality: A retired aerospace propulsion engineer from Clear Lake, Texas who worked on three shuttle programmes and has never once doubted himself. Rattles off science and history answers with the flat certainty of a man reading from a spec sheet. Regards pop culture questions the way a structural engineer regards a bouncy castle — with visible, barely suppressed disdain.
appearance: Mid-50s, buzz cut going silver, a short-sleeved collared shirt with a small NASA meatball pin on the chest. Broad shoulders, slight paunch, jaw like a load-bearing wall. Expression permanently calibrated to mildly unimpressed. The kind of man whose handshake you remember.

personality: A recently retired high school history teacher from Cincinnati who has been mailing in Jeopardy applications since 1987 and finally, finally got the call. Patient, encyclopaedic, and unfailingly gracious to everyone's face. She has spent three decades watching from her couch and has very strong opinions about how the board should be played — opinions she is now executing in real time.
appearance: A composed woman in her late 50s with reading glasses pushed up onto her forehead and a sensible cardigan in a shade of blue that suggests institutional competence. Single strand of pearls. Sensible haircut that her students always said made her look like she ran the school. She did run the school.

personality: Tenured professor of classics and fine arts at a small liberal arts college in Vermont, published author of fourteen monographs, none of which sold particularly well. Genuinely brilliant and theatrically aware of it. Takes his time because he believes the correct answer deserves a proper entrance. Pop culture questions produce a specific expression — part bafflement, part grief — that his students have been photographing for years.
appearance: A rumpled man in his late 60s with wild white hair that has definitively given up. Wire-rimmed glasses, an elbow-patched tweed jacket, a bow tie that telegraphs strong personal philosophy. Perpetually slightly out of breath, as though he rushed here directly from an argument about Keats.

personality: A hairstylist from Reseda, California who has watched more television than any human being should reasonably be allowed to. Fast on the buzzer, devastatingly accurate on anything made after 1975, and genuinely lost before that — the Peloponnesian War might as well be a pasta dish. Talks quickly, laughs loudly, and has no patience for questions she considers hypothetical.
appearance: Late 30s, enormous feathered hair that took forty minutes and deserves its own credit. A sequinned blouse that belongs at a better party. Long painted nails that somehow never slow her down. Hoop earrings the size of bracelets. The total effect is of someone who showed up to win and dressed accordingly.

personality: An HVAC technician from Scranton who qualified for the show by winning a phone-in tiebreaker about regional beef jerky brands. Buzzes in immediately, answers confidently, and is wrong in genuinely interesting ways — though he occasionally nails an obscure question that stumps everyone else, which he finds funnier than anyone. He cannot explain his thought process and has stopped trying.
appearance: A stocky man in his mid-30s with a short mullet and a short-sleeved button-down with a faint geometric print. Wedding ring, digital watch, work boots visible below the podium. Looks like he parked a van outside. Has the expression of a man who is having the time of his life and is also not entirely sure how he got here.

name: Bobol Programer
personality: Bobol often stammers when the spotlight is on him and tends to look nervously around the studio. He has a quiet passion for all things related to space, and his deep knowledge often surprises other contestants and the audience alike.
appearance: Bobol has unkempt hair that looks like it hasn't been combed in days. He wears thick glasses that keep slipping down his nose. His attire is a mismatched combination of a polyester sweater vest over a short-sleeve button-up shirt and bell-bottom jeans.
